prevalence.source: ICMR/NFHS-5 (National Family Health Survey-5, 2019-2021 extrapolated to 2024)
prevalence.trend: Increasing by 1-2% annually due to rising air pollution and urbanization

incidence.source: WHO Global Burden of Disease Study 2021; ICMR reports on acute respiratory infections

burden.dalyPerCapita: 0.015 DALYs per capita (respiratory infections contribute ~2% of total DALYs)
burden.mortalityRate: 0.1-0.2 deaths per 100,000 population (mostly complications in vulnerable groups)
burden.source: Global Burden of Disease (GBD) 2021; Indian Council of Medical Research (ICMR) 2023 estimates

riskFactors: Air pollution (PM2.5 levels exceed WHO limits in 70% of cities, increasing risk by 20-30%)
Seasonal variation (peaks in winter and monsoon, up to 40% higher incidence)
Overcrowding and poor ventilation in urban slums
Tobacco smoke exposure and low socioeconomic status
vulnerablePopulations: Residents in high-pollution areas like Indo-Gangetic Plain
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical: Symptoms including sore throat, nasal congestion, cough, fever <38.5°C lasting <7 days; confirmed by rapid antigen tests for streptococcal or viral causes if needed (IDSA guidelines adapted for India)

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: Limited routine screening (20-30% in primary health centers; higher in urban areas)
publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: ~60-80 million annual cases (based on incidence rates)
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: 70-80% symptom resolution with primary care; antibiotic stewardship improving to reduce resistance

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Symptom-based in rural areas; syndromic approach per ICMR guidelines, with emphasis on ruling out COVID-19 co-infections
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Symptomatic relief (analgesics, decongestants); antibiotics only for bacterial confirmation (e.g., amoxicillin per NTEP); supportive care in monsoon seasons
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: High air pollution in northern India (e.g., Delhi AQI >300 exacerbates cases by 25%); rural access gaps (only 40% PHC coverage); seasonal surges overwhelm facilities
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Robust immunization programs (influenza vaccine coverage rising to 15% in high-risk groups); community health workers (ASHAs) for early detection; declining mortality due to improved hygiene campaigns
